prompt: Programmatic composition brief: create a single-frame magazine-opening image about global mammal biomass where the viewer can feel that wild mammals have been squeezed to the margin. No axes, no legend, no labels, no standard chart. Use a dark, oppressive upper mass made of human and livestock silhouettes occupying 95% of the visual field, pressing diagonally downward like a landslide. Reserve a small lower refuge of light for only five luminous wild mammal silhouettes, making the imbalance emotionally obvious before it is counted. Editorial, tactile, somber, high-contrast, restrained palette, not infographic, not cute, not photorealistic.
dataset_name: Global mammal biomass by group (humans, livestock & pets, wild mammals)
insight: Humans and livestock now make up 95% of the world's mammal biomass, leaving wild mammals just 5%.
